_fixture_note: Minimal but realistic ComparisonData for testing scripts/ingest-apriori.py. Fictional client 'fixturo' — not a real product. All numbers are illustrative. Schema mirrors apriori_landing's ComparisonData type as observed in src/app/demo/<client>/page.tsx.

variants.label: Control
variants.description: Baseline onboarding — single-step signup with email + password.

variants.label: Variant 1
variants.description: Adds social-proof banner above the form: 'Joined by 50,000+ teams.'

variants.label: Variant 2
variants.description: Replaces password field with magic-link email auth. Removes friction.

modifications: Test magic-link delivery time across email providers — sub-30s target.
verdict.verdict_text: Variant 2 is the highest-converting flow at 48% (vs 28% Control). Magic-link auth removes the password-creation friction that the simulation flagged as the dominant drop-off cause.

resolved.name: Password creation friction
resolved.description: Control required users to create + confirm a password meeting strength rules; 23% abandoned at this step. Variant 2's magic-link flow removed this entirely.

monologues.control: Special character, number, capital, minimum 8 chars — I just want to look at this product.
monologues.a: Same password rigamarole even with the social proof. I'll come back later.
monologues.b: Magic link to my inbox. Done. I'm in.

introduced.name: Email delivery dependence
introduced.description: Variant 2's magic-link adds a hard dependency on email-delivery latency. Some users in the simulation flagged that they 'didn't trust waiting' for an email.

monologue_evidence.persona_archetype: Skeptical early adopter who reads ToS

monologues.b: If I can't see what I'm signing up for until they email me, I'd rather just not sign up.

summaries.control: Email + password fields, 'Create Account' button. Standard form, no social proof. Clinical.
summaries.a: Same form, with a banner above: 'Joined by 50,000+ teams' + small avatar stack. Form unchanged.
summaries.b: Email-only field, 'Send magic link' CTA. No password. Trust badge: 'Magic link expires in 10 min.'

persona_journeys.name: Time-Pressed Founder
persona_journeys.narrative: Bounced off Control during password creation. Variant 1's social proof was nice but the password barrier was still there. Variant 2's magic link converted in under 30 seconds — exactly the experience they want.

persona_journeys.name: Cautious Evaluator
persona_journeys.narrative: Liked the social-proof banner in Variant 1 — wanted to see who else uses this. Magic link in Variant 2 felt unfamiliar; preferred the explicit password flow.

persona_journeys.name: Privacy Hawk
persona_journeys.narrative: Distrusted the magic link — felt it was an extra step that put control in the email provider's hands. Stuck with Control's explicit signup.

segment_verdicts.narrative: Magic link wins decisively — sub-30-second activation matches the segment's preference for speed.
Wants familiar flow + social proof. Magic link feels unfamiliar to this segment.
Prefers explicit auth they control. Magic link reads as an extra trust dependency.

friction_provenance.friction: Password creation friction — strength rules + confirmation step
No social proof / trust signals on the auth page
Email delivery dependence — distrust for users who can't see the product first

recommendations.recommendation: Ship Variant 2 with magic-link delivery SLA monitoring
recommendations.rationale: 20pt overall conversion lift. Privacy Hawk segment (n=8) is small — the cost of dropping them is less than the gain across other segments.
recommendations.effort_estimate: Already implemented in the test variant
recommendations.success_metric: Activation rate at 45%+ over 4 weeks; magic-link p90 delivery < 30s

recommendations.recommendation: Pilot passkey auth as a Variant 3 to retain Privacy Hawk segment
recommendations.rationale: Passkeys give Privacy Hawks the explicit-control feel of password without the friction.

recommended_next_test.name: Variant 2 + Passkey fallback
recommended_next_test.hypothesis: Adding a passkey option alongside magic link will recover the Privacy Hawk segment without sacrificing magic-link's speed advantage for Time-Pressed Founders.

risks_to_monitor: Magic-link p90 delivery time across email providers — if > 30s, Time-Pressed Founder conversion may regress.
Privacy Hawk segment may shrink the addressable market if their objection isn't resolved by V3.
